title: refund — model relationships
description: Two owned entities. Every row is tenant-scoped (cascades on tenant delete). Refund is the aggregate root; RefundLine belongs to a Refund. Every outbound FK stays same-tenant per tenancy-columns.md §5. NO application_id / organization_id / region_id / branch_id / scope_node_id on any owned row — all cascade through tenant_id. Refund references Payment + Invoice + CreditNote + Transaction with RESTRICT semantics — financial records cannot orphan reversal records.

internal.description: A refund can have zero (full refund of whole payment) or many (partial / prorated) refund_lines. Sum of refund_lines.amount_cents must equal refund.amount_cents (observer-enforced).
CASCADE on delete. Refund lines are pivot rows co-terminous with the parent refund.

outbound.description: Every refund is tenant-scoped per tenancy-columns.md §3.
RESTRICT on delete. Payment cannot be deleted while any refund references it — financial records are 7y-retained + cannot orphan reversal records.
Populated when FinalizeRefundJob step 2 completes CreditNoteIssuer::createFromRefund. NULL until then. RESTRICT prevents orphan credit notes.
Populated when FinalizeRefundJob step 1 completes TransactionRecorder::recordOffset. NULL until then. RESTRICT prevents orphan ledger entries.
The auth actor who initiated the refund. RESTRICT — audit trail cannot orphan. User erasure triggers RedactCustomerFromRefundsOnUserErased hook 90d post-erasure.
The approver. NULL when refund auto-approved (no human approver). RESTRICT.
The rejector. NULL unless refund transitioned to rejected. RESTRICT.
The invoice line being refunded. RESTRICT — invoice line cannot be deleted while refund_lines reference it.

inbound.description: Downstream modules attach in later waves:
examples: chargeback (Wave 4 sibling) — a chargeback resolution decision to accept via refund creates a Refund with reason='chargeback_prevention'. The chargeback row references the resulting refund_id for cross-lookup.
growth::marketing::MarketingEvent (Wave 5) — RefundSucceeded fires a negative-value marketing event; the marketing_event row carries refund_id for revenue attribution reversal.
growth::referrals::ReferralReward (Wave 5) — RefundClawbackReferral cascades to referrals; the reversed reward references refund_id via metadata.clawback_refund_id.
finance::coupon::CouponRedemption — RefundClawbackCoupon cascades to coupon; the reversed redemption's clawback_reason='refund' is set + coupon.usage_count decremented.
inbound.note: No inbound relations in Wave 4 beyond chargeback bridge — this module is a finance-tier terminal aggregate (initiated by customers OR admins directly OR by chargeback module on dispute-accept).

notes: Refund does NOT carry `application_id`. Refunds cascade through the tenant's application per tenancy-columns.md §2. Cross-application refunds are non-goals — a Sports-app refund cannot reference a Marketplace-app payment.
RefundLine composite unique on (tenant_id, refund_id, invoice_line_id) prevents double-refunding a single invoice line within one refund.

Cross-refund invariant enforced at observer level (not via a DB constraint — the check would require a subquery that's hard to express as a table constraint): sum of amount_cents across all NON-cancelled + NON-rejected + NON-failed refunds for a given payment_id must not exceed payment.amount_cents.

credit_note_id + transaction_id are nullable during pending states. Once refund transitions to succeeded, both MUST be set (FinalizeRefundJob populates them). If FinalizeRefundJob fails, the refund transitions to provider_error + credit_note_id / transaction_id stay NULL until admin runs refund:generate-credit-note.

user_id references (initiated_by / approved_by / rejected_by / reversed_by / cancelled_by) are RESTRICT — user cannot be hard-deleted while refunds reference them. UserErased triggers the redaction hook (90d post-erasure) to replace user_ids with a hashed placeholder while retaining the audit trail.
